cases.question: Which active accounts have the highest balances? Show the account number and current balance for the top 20.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account {status: 'Active'})
RETURN a.accountNumber AS accountNumber, a.balance AS balance
ORDER BY a.balance DESC

cases.question: What does transaction activity look like across 2025? Break down the count and total value by transaction type.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)
WHERE t.date >= date('2025-01-01') AND t.date <= date('2025-12-31')
RETURN t.type AS transactionType,
       count(t) AS transactionCount,
       round(sum(t.amount), 2) AS totalValue
ORDER BY totalValue DESC

cases.question: What is the average, highest, and total number of transactions for each transaction status (pending, completed, etc.)?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)
RETURN
  t.status                        AS status,
  count(t)                        AS totalTransactions,
  avg(t.amount)                   AS avgAmount,
  max(t.amount)                   AS highestAmount
ORDER BY totalTransactions DESC

cases.question: Which accounts are indirectly linked to each other through chains of shared personal details? I want to see account pairs that are connected through two to four intermediate links.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{2,4}-(b:Account)
WHERE elementId(a) < elementId(b)
RETURN a.accountID           AS accountA,
       b.accountID           AS accountB,
       a.louvainCommunity    AS communityA,
       b.louvainCommunity    AS communityB,
       a.status              AS statusA,
       b.status              AS statusB
ORDER BY communityA, accountA, accountB
LIMIT 100

cases.question: What is the shortest chain of linked accounts connecting a savings account to a checking account?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH p = SHORTEST 1
  (s:Account WHERE s.accountType = 'Savings')
  -[:TRANSACTED_TO]-{1,}
  (c:Account WHERE c.accountType = 'Checking')
RETURN
  s.accountID        AS startAccount,
  c.accountID        AS endAccount,
  [n IN nodes(p) | n.accountID] AS accountChain,
  length(p)          AS chainLength

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Which customers sit at the center of the most financial connections in our network? For each, show whether they have any open alerts or investigations.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (c:Customer)
WITH c,
     COLLECT {
         MATCH (c)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(:Account)-[:HAS_ALERT]->(al:Alert)-[:HAS_STATUS]->(als:AlertStatus)
         WHERE als.status IN ['open', 'investigating']
         RETURN {id: al.alertId, severity: al.severity, status: als.status}
     } AS activeAlerts
ORDER BY c.betweenness DESC
LIMIT 20
RETURN
    c.name                                              AS customer,
    c.customerID                                        AS customerID,
    round(c.betweenness, 4)                             AS betweenness,
    c.fraudFlag                                         AS flagged,
    size(activeAlerts) > 0                              AS hasActiveAlerts,
    size(activeAlerts)                                  AS activeAlertCount,
    [a IN activeAlerts | a.severity + ' / ' + a.status] AS alertDetails

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Which transactions are unusually large compared to other transactions of the same type? Show transactions that exceed the average for their category.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (t:Transaction)
WITH t.type AS txType, avg(t.amount) AS avgAmount
WITH collect({txType: txType, avgAmount: avgAmount}) AS typeAvgs
MATCH (t:Transaction)
WITH t, [x IN typeAvgs WHERE x.txType = t.type | x.avgAmount][0] AS typeAvg
WHERE t.amount > typeAvg
RETURN t.transactionID,
       t.type,
       t.status,
       t.date,
       t.amount,
       round(typeAvg, 2)            AS typeAverage,
       round(t.amount / typeAvg, 2) AS timesAboveAverage
ORDER BY timesAboveAverage DESC
LIMIT 25

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

       c.pagerank           AS pagerank,

       c.louvainCommunity   AS fraudCluster,

ORDER BY score DESC, c.pagerank DESC

cases.question: Find pairs of accounts that share personal details where at least one account belongs to a customer flagged for fraud. Who owns these accounts?
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a1:Account)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-(a2:Account)
WHERE elementId(a1) < elementId(a2)
MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a1)
MATCH (c2:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(a2)
WHERE c1.fraudFlag = 'True' OR c2.fraudFlag = 'True'
RETURN c1.name          AS owner1,
       a1.accountID     AS account1,
       a1.accountType   AS accountType1,
       a1.status        AS status1,
       c1.fraudFlag     AS fraudFlagged1,
       c2.name          AS owner2,
       a2.accountID     AS account2,
       a2.accountType   AS accountType2,
       a2.status        AS status2,
       c2.fraudFlag     AS fraudFlagged2
ORDER BY c1.fraudFlag DESC, c2.fraudFlag DESC, c1.name, c2.name

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Which accounts received unusually large sums of money within a single month in 2025? Flag any account that received more than $10,000 in one calendar month.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account)<-[:RECEIVED]-(t:Transaction)
WHERE t.date.year = 2025
  AND t.status = 'Completed'
WITH a,
     t.date.year  AS year,
     t.date.month AS month,
     sum(t.amount) AS monthlyReceived
WHERE monthlyReceived > 10000
RETURN a.accountID,
       a.accountNumber,
       a.accountType,
       a.status,
       year,
       month,
       round(monthlyReceived, 2) AS monthlyReceived,
       a.louvainCommunity AS fraudCluster
ORDER BY monthlyReceived DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Are there customers in the same fraud network cluster who are also sharing phone numbers or email addresses with each other? This could indicate coordinated activity.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
CALL {
  MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:USES_EMAIL]->(e:Email)<-[:USES_EMAIL]-(c2:Customer)
  WHERE c1.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL
    AND c1.louvainCommunity = c2.louvainCommunity
    AND c1.customerID < c2.customerID
  RETURN c1, c2, 'email' AS identifierType, e.email AS identifierValue

  UNION ALL

  MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:USES_PHONE]->(p:PhoneNumber)<-[:USES_PHONE]-(c2:Customer)
  WHERE c1.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL
    AND c1.louvainCommunity = c2.louvainCommunity
    AND c1.customerID < c2.customerID
  RETURN c1, c2, 'phone' AS identifierType, p.phoneNumber AS identifierValue
}
WITH c1, c2,
     [r IN collect({t: identifierType, v: identifierValue}) WHERE r.t = 'email' | r.v] AS sharedEmails,
     [r IN collect({t: identifierType, v: identifierValue}) WHERE r.t = 'phone' | r.v] AS sharedPhones
RETURN c1.louvainCommunity        AS fraudCluster,
       c1.name                    AS customer1,
       c2.name                    AS customer2,
       c1.fraudFlag               AS customer1Flagged,
       c2.fraudFlag               AS customer2Flagged,
       sharedEmails,
       sharedPhones,
       size(sharedEmails) + size(sharedPhones) AS totalSharedIdentifiers
ORDER BY fraudCluster, totalSharedIdentifiers DESC

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected

cases.question: Among the most influential accounts in our network, which pairs are most directly connected through shared identity links? Show the tightest connections first.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (a:Account)
WITH a ORDER BY a.pagerank DESC LIMIT 20
WITH collect(a) AS topAccounts
UNWIND topAccounts AS a
UNWIND topAccounts AS b
WITH a, b
WHERE elementId(a) < elementId(b)
MATCH path = SHORTEST 1 (a)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]+(b)
RETURN
  a.accountID                        AS accountA,
  round(a.pagerank, 4)               AS pagerankA,
  b.accountID                        AS accountB,
  round(b.pagerank, 4)               AS pagerankB,
  length(path)                       AS sharedIdHops,
  round(a.pagerank + b.pagerank, 4)  AS combinedInfluence
ORDER BY sharedIdHops ASC, combinedInfluence DESC
LIMIT 20

cases.question: Are there active accounts sending money through multiple intermediate accounts before reaching a destination — a pattern that could indicate layering in money laundering? Show account pairs with multiple such routing paths.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH path = ACYCLIC (source:Account {status: 'Active'})-[:TRANSACTED_TO]-{3,5}->(dest:Account {status: 'Active'})
WHERE source <> dest
  AND ALL(n IN nodes(path)[1..-1] WHERE n.status = 'Active')
WITH source, dest,
     count(path)        AS pathCount,
     min(length(path))  AS shortestHops,
     max(length(path))  AS longestHops
WHERE pathCount > 1
OPTIONAL MATCH (c1:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(source)
OPTIONAL MATCH (c2:Customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(dest)
RETURN
  source.accountID                                  AS sourceAccount,
  coalesce(c1.name, '?')                            AS sourceCustomer,
  dest.accountID                                    AS destAccount,
  coalesce(c2.name, '?')                            AS destCustomer,
  pathCount                                         AS layeringPathCount,
  shortestHops                                      AS minHops,
  longestHops                                       AS maxHops,
  (source.louvainCommunity = dest.louvainCommunity) AS sameFraudCluster
ORDER BY pathCount DESC
LIMIT 25

cases.question: Mark all customers who belong to the same network cluster as known fraudsters as 'suspicious due to network association'. Process them in small batches to avoid overloading the system.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
MATCH (fraudster:Customer {fraudFlag: 'True'})
WHERE fraudster.louvainCommunity IS NOT NULL
WITH COLLECT(DISTINCT fraudster.louvainCommunity) AS fraudCommunities

MATCH (c:Customer)
WHERE c.louvainCommunity IN fraudCommunities
  AND c.fraudFlag <> 'True'

CALL (c) {
  SET c.suspicionReason = 'suspicious due to network association'
} IN TRANSACTIONS OF 100 ROWS

gate_details.reason: Execution error: {neo4j_code: Neo.DatabaseError.Transaction.TransactionStartFailed} {message: A query with 'CALL { ... } IN TRANSACTIONS' can only be executed in an implicit transaction, but tried to execute in an explicit transaction.} {gql_status: 25N17} {gql_status_description: error: invalid transaction state - implicit transaction required. The attempted operation requires an implicit transaction.}

cases.question: Search for customers with the surname 'Poole' and trace how many accounts in our system are linked to their accounts — directly or through up to three degrees of shared identity.
cases.generated_cypher:
CYPHER 25
CALL db.index.fulltext.queryNodes('customerNames', 'Poole') YIELD node AS customer, score
WITH customer
WHERE customer.name ENDS WITH ' Poole' OR customer.name = 'Poole'
MATCH (customer)-[:HAS_ACCOUNT]->(seed:Account)
WITH collect(DISTINCT seed) AS seedAccounts,
     collect(DISTINCT customer.name) AS customerNames
UNWIND seedAccounts AS seed
MATCH (seed)-[:SHARED_IDENTIFIERS]-{1,3}(linked:Account)
WHERE NOT linked IN seedAccounts
WITH customerNames, seedAccounts, collect(DISTINCT linked) AS linkedAccounts
RETURN
  customerNames,
  size(seedAccounts)                              AS ownAccountCount,
  size(linkedAccounts)                            AS linkedAccountCount,
  size(seedAccounts) + size(linkedAccounts)       AS totalReachableAccounts

gate_details.reason: No deprecated syntax or operators detected
